name: Online Co-op
description: Online Co-op is a social or networked mechanic that coordinates multiple players within shared rules.
design_notes: Online Co-op should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For multiplayer mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use online co-op as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect online co-op to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: Players feel coordinated, competitive, or socially expressive within shared rules.

parameters.name: player_count
parameters.description: Number of players supported by the mechanic.

parameters.name: sync_rate
parameters.description: Frequency at which state updates are exchanged or resolved.

parameters.name: failure_timeout
parameters.description: Time before the mechanic handles disconnects or missing responses.

edge_cases: Online Co-op is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Online Co-op overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Online Co-op runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Online Co-op state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Online Co-op plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Online Co-op produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Online Co-op strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Online Co-op becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Online Co-op with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Online Co-op as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Online Co-op with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Online Co-op as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
